commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: Even though many have fallen away, those whom God foreknew have remained in the promise of the law. For those who have accepted Christ as he was promised in the law have remained in the law, but those who rejected Christ have fallen away from it. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: The election of which the apostle speaks is according to grace, not merit.

commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: The “remnant” refers to the Jews who have believed in Christ. Many of them did believe in the days of the apostles, and even today there are some converts, though very few.

commentaries.author: Eusebius of Caesarea
commentaries.content: The remnant of the Jews has proclaimed the sign of the Lord to all the Gentiles and has joined to God in one people, drawn to him, the souls of the Gentiles which are brought out of destruction to the knowledge of the Lord.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
